sanskrit: अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः । शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥
transliteration: anāditvān nirguṇatvāt paramātmāyam avyayaḥ | śarīrastho ’pi kaunteya na karoti na lipyate ||
literal_meaning: Because of beginninglessness and absence of qualities, this Supreme Self is immutable; though situated in the body, O son of Kuntī, it neither acts nor is tainted.
contextual_meaning: The observing self (sākṣī) is prior to narrative, unconditioned by personality traits or mood states; therefore it cannot be pathologized, blamed, or redeemed—it simply witnesses.
psychological_state: Dis-identification from rumination and somatic distress; shift from egoic enmeshment to meta-cognitive awareness.
trigger_scenario: A client in therapy realizes 'I am not my anxiety' and watches the panic rise and fall without being consumed by it.
anchor_text: You are the silent witness within, untouched by action or stain.
